steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with 'Home to roughly 710,000 people,' — a participial phrase that must logically modify the subject of the main clause. Only a location can be 'home to' people.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the city of Riga is...': The city is a location, so it can logically be home to 710,000 people. The modifier attaches correctly. ✓
  • Choice B — fragment structure: The construction lacks a proper main verb, producing a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — 'an impressive 35 percent...': A percentage cannot be 'home to' people — dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — 'an impressive 35 percent...': Same issue as C; the subject is still a percentage, not a place. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
